my_thoughts: 'I guess I'll have to figure it out'—grudging but acceptance. Five turns from 'you should tell me' to 'I'll figure it out.' Each turn: student demanded authority transfer, teacher refused and returned it. The consistency mattered—couldn't grab authority at decision level, capability level, or method level. All blocked. Result: student has their own authority back. Grudging ('I guess') but real. Five-turn pattern: refuse dependency at every level until student accepts autonomy. Clean teaching arc.
accumulated_insight: T5 (Final): Student accepts 'I guess I'll have to figure it out'—resignation containing acceptance. Five-turn arc: demands decision (T1) → claims incapacity (T2) → names desire (T3) → seeks method (T4) → accepts responsibility (T5). Teacher consistently refused authority at every level: decision, capability, implementation. Student moved from passive omnipotence to grudging autonomy. Teaching: blocked dependency, returned agency repeatedly until accepted.
visible_response:
I’m glad to help you.
But I have to accept I can’t help you while you give up on yourself.
If you don’t have the energy to do this, I can’t do it by myself.
